context_summary: M1 ship-gate closed (Phase 7, 2026-07-12). M2 in flight: Phase 8-11 closed; forensics housekeeping (STATE/HANDOFF/PROJECT/ROADMAP/999.archive) committed 2026-07-22. Phase 12 (CNR alerting / dissemination) entered execution. Sub-wave (a) ntfy container ITERATED 9 ROUNDS across 2026-07-23 in-container-bashscript bootstrap attempts before the operator terminated with 'kill the per-cmd-pattern-matching approach; use sealed bind-mount'. Sub-wave (a) is now LOCKED on the sealed-bind-mount architecture (ADR-017 sealed-bind-mount addendum). Phase 12 continues with sub-waves (b)+(c)+(d) on the rebuilt foundation; (f) Phase 13 body-wiring is a bundled sub-wave. Phase 12 sub-wave (a) subsequently PIVOTED 2026-07-23 to Dockerfile pre-bake + BuildKit secrets (ADR-018); sealed-bind-mount substrate (commit 5c52056 + audit findings) archived to docs/planning/_archived/phase-12-sealed-bind-mount-attempt/. Phase 12 continues on the new ADR-018 foundation.

phase_11_closeout.summary: Phase 11 (SOCMINT + Arctic) closed 2026-07-22. Waves 1-6 complete: ADR-014 SOCMINT legal/ToS, discipline + Admiralty reliability provenance schema, MCP adapters for Telegram (Telethon) + BarentsWatch (AIS), on-demand translation (Phase 999.1 closed via Wave 4), YouTube local faster-whisper transcript (Wave 5). ACLED gate keeps unlicensed data out. Full pytest suite 518 passed / 61 skipped (default); integration suite 579/0/0.

completed_waves.name: Advanced Media — YouTube local faster-whisper transcription

999.2.rationale: Dedup threshold calibration on larger corpus — same-day R2 spike was too narrow. Open into the first M2 phase that runs controlled off-topic-vs-same-event evaluation.

999.3.status: CLOSED 2026-07-12 (mE5-large threshold re-validated at T*=0.92; live --corpus-from-postgres GO at T*=0.92)

999.4.status: CLOSED 2026-07-22 (shipped as Phase 10 Wave 4 — verify_language_coverage() in libs/contracts/_verify.py)

scope_lock.decision_rationale: Sub-wave (a) ntfy container ITERATED 9 ROUNDS across in-container ACL bootstrap patterns before the operator terminated with 'kill the per-cmd-pattern-matching approach; use sealed bind-mount just like other services (DLQ consumer pattern)'. The architectural shift to sealed-bind-mount is the correct foundation; further iteration on (a)'s bootstrap is brittle-system-thrashing with no terminus without changing the architecture. Phase 12 ships (b)+(c)+(d) on the rebuilt foundation.
scope_lock.evidence: Validation pass 2026-07-23: pytest 568 passed full suite (1 passed in tests/test_ntfy_health.py + 6 expected-failures pending `make ntfy-seed`), mypy --strict clean, black --check clean, docker compose config ntfy parses cleanly, server.yml yaml.safe_load returns all 5 required keys (auth-default-access=deny-all, auth-file=/etc/ntfy/auth.db matches bind), seed script unit checks HELPER-SPLIT-PASS + OVERRIDE-OK + --help works, code-reviewer-m3 verbatim: SHIP with 3 minor polish items.
scope_lock.pivot_rationale: Operator reviewed the sealed-bind-mount architecture (commit 5c52056 + audit findings) and concluded the host-side Python prelude (scripts/seed_ntfy_sealed.py) + sealed config file (configs/ntfy-sealed/server.yml) + bind :ro pattern was overcomplicated for a single-binary stack. Pivoted to Dockerfile pre-bake + BuildKit secrets (ADR-018): 1 custom Dockerfile + 1 minimal compose change replaces the 298-line Python prelude + custom-named config + env overrides.
scope_lock.preserved_audit_value: The earlier audit findings remain as historical record: iteration_history (9 rounds + 4 post-r9 fixes), brittle_decisions (8 entries), root_cause (multiplicative shell/YAML escape coupling). The lesson generalizes: brittle-system thrashing has no terminus without a widely different substrate. ADR-018 replaces the brittle substrate; the lesson generalizes.

iteration_history.blocker: command: serve had no ACL bootstrap
ntfy image ENTRYPOINT is /ntfy, so 'sh' was treated as an arg → 'No help topic for sh'
YAML \" escape + docker-compose $$ escape interfered; grep received wrong arg, exited 2 silently
awk output-format coupling to ntfy CLI tab-output layout; silent fall-through on format drift
iteration_history.fix: added --json flag + grep against JSON output

iteration_history.blocker: { grep -v 'already exists' || [ $? -eq 1 ]; } sentinel returned non-zero on first-run success; set -e aborted bootstrap
{ X && Y } in shell is short-circuit; if ! { X && Y } parses as (!X) && Y, NOT !(X && Y) — FATAL fired on SUCCESSFUL bootstrap
operator overrode: brittle to debug, not the right pattern at all
iteration_history.fix: SWITCH TO SEALED-BIND-MOUNT architecture (host-side scripts/seed_ntfy_sealed.py via docker run --rm; bind :ro for server.yml + auth.db; bind :rw for /var/cache/ntfy)

iteration_history.blocker: _sealed_already_valid() probe called `ntfy user list` WITHOUT --auth-file, so it ran against the ephemeral container's empty default auth-db path and silently returned exit 0
_env_or_default(env) or 'producer' short-circuit because _env_or_default returns 'changeme' (truthy), so 'or producer' never fires
server.yml auth-file pointed at /etc/ntfy-sealed/auth.db but docker-compose mounted at /etc/ntfy/auth.db → ntfy read missing file → deny-all collapses to unrestricted
iteration_history.fix: server.yml auth-file updated to /etc/ntfy/auth.db to match bind

iteration_history.blocker: AUTH_DB.stat().st_size < 1024 sentinel oversize; freshly-bcrypt'd auth.db is ~600 bytes; sentinel was non-load-bearing
iteration_history.fix: dropped size check; SQLite probe via ntfy user list is the real gate

brittle_decisions.brittleness: Tool escape sequences (YAML \\, docker-compose $$, shell $?, brace-group precedence) compose in un-debuggable ways inside a one-liner. Each fix exposed a new coupling layer.
Couples to ntfy's specific user-list tabular output format. Any CLI format change = silent failure.
Relies on the specific shell semantic that [ $? -eq 1 ] evaluates non-zero on success. Counter-intuitive and easy to break.
Shell parses as (!X) && Y, NOT !(X && Y). Easy to confuse in the wild.
Freshly-bcrypt'd auth.db is ~600 bytes; size threshold was schematic and non-load-bearing.
_env_or_default returns 'changeme' (truthy), so `or fallback` never fires. Usernames silently became 'changeme' not 'producer'.
Tied to brittle bootstrap that needed fast-fail on FATAL exit 3. Once kill-bootstrap removed the failure mode, restart went back to unless-stopped per ADR-017 Decision 4.

brittle_decisions.decision: str_replace multi-line edits with embedded escape sequences
brittle_decisions.brittleness: Tool transmitted POSIX case-glob '' as Markdown-escaped, causing multi-line replace to fail. Same fix had to be re-applied via basher+python heredoc at least 4 times.

phase_12_subwave_a_audit_findings.root_cause: Nine rounds of debugging all attempted to fix the SAME pattern: running ntfy user-add/access CLI commands inside a one-shot container orchestration shell on every container start. The pattern's failure modes are multiplicative because YAML/compose/shell escapes compose in ways that are NOT debuggable from outside the container at each round. The sealed-bind-mount architecture replaces runtime bootstrap with a host-side one-time prep, so the container just 'ntfy serve's. Brittle-system thrashing ends once the architecture is right; iteration on the bootstrap alone has no terminus.

rebuilt_foundation.architecture: sealed-bind-mount (host-side scripts/seed_ntfy_sealed.py via docker run --rm against binwiederhier/ntfy:latest; bind :ro for ./configs/ntfy-sealed/server.yml:/etc/ntfy/server.yml and ./configs/ntfy-sealed/auth.db:/etc/ntfy/auth.db; bind :rw for ./data/ntfy-cache:/var/cache/ntfy)
preserved_decisions: ADR-017 Decision 1 — split producer (write-only) + reader (read-only) users on cnr-cat-i*
ADR-017 Decision 2 — ship on binwiederhier/ntfy:latest (dev-acceptable; re-pin to :2.27.0+ when published per ADR-018)
ADR-017 Decision 3 — dev-only log-warn on 'changeme' passwords (no fail-fast); production gate is a future ADR
ADR-017 Decision 4 — restart: unless-stopped (unbounded; no boot-time bootstrap to retry)

validation_evidence.pytest_test_ntfy_health: 1 passed (test_server_yaml_schema_valid) + 6 expected-failures (5 docker-dependent + 1 needs auth.db populated)
validation_evidence.docker_compose_config_ntfy_block: parses cleanly; sealed binds :ro, cache :rw, restart unless-stopped, start_period 15s
validation_evidence.yaml_safe_load_server_yml: 5 required keys present (base-url, auth-file, auth-default-access, user-db, cache-db); auth-default-access=deny-all; auth-file=/etc/ntfy/auth.db matches bind
validation_evidence.seed_script_unit_checks: HELPER-SPLIT-PASS (producer_user='producer' NOT 'changeme'); OVERRIDE-OK (env wins when set); --help works
validation_evidence.code_reviewer_m3_verdict: SHIP with 3 minor polish items (FATAL recovery hint, telemetry exit code, defensive fetchone handling)

Do NOT add any entrypoint:/command: override to the ntfy service block. ADR-017 sealed-bind-mount is the contract.
Do NOT hand-edit configs/ntfy-sealed/{server.yml, auth.db} mid-phase. Re-seed via `make ntfy-seed -- --force` only when an operator changes .env credentials.
Do NOT resurrect the retired ntfy-bootstrap Makefile target. ntfy-seed replaces it.

tests/test_ntfy_health.py — 2 new sealed-bundle tests (test_sealed_artifacts_present + test_server_yaml_schema_valid) gate against re-introducing the in-container bootstrap.
Makefile — ntfy-seed target is the only legitimate way to regenerate the sealed bundle; ntfy-up auto-fires ntfy-seed if the bundle is missing.
docs/planning/phase-12-subwave-a-audit.md — operator-facing summary (~80 lines) for one-read post-mortem.

phase_12_subwave_a_final_architecture.architecture: Dockerfile pre-bake + BuildKit secrets (multi-stage). apps/ntfy/Dockerfile builder runs `ntfy user add` + `ntfy access` against /etc/ntfy/auth.db with 3 secrets mounted at /run/secrets/*; final image is the unmodified ntfy official image + COPY --from=builder /etc/ntfy/auth.db /etc/ntfy/auth.db (only the bcrypt-only db is layered in).
phase_12_subwave_a_final_architecture.compose_change: docker-compose.yml ntfy block: image: -> build: (context: ./apps/ntfy + secrets: [ntfy_producer_password, ntfy_reader_password, ntfy_topic_prefix]); top-level secrets: block maps BuildKit IDs to .env variable names via the {environment: ...} source syntax (requires Compose v2.20.0+). The runtime cache bind (./data/ntfy-cache:/var/cache/ntfy:rw) is preserved.
preserved_decisions: ADR-017 Decision 1 (HIGH) — split producer (write-only) + reader (read-only) users on cnr-cat-i*
ADR-017 Decision 2 (MEDIUM) — ship on binwiederhier/ntfy:latest (dev-acceptable; pre-release blocker for production)
ADR-017 Decision 3 (MEDIUM) — dev-only log-warn on `changeme` passwords (no fail-fast; future ADR covers production gate)
ADR-017 Decision 4 (LOW) — restart: unless-stopped (no boot-time bootstrap to retry)
phase_12_subwave_a_final_architecture.plaintext_safety: Plaintext passwords NEVER appear in image layers — BuildKit secrets are mounted at /run/secrets/* inside the builder stage only; `docker history` shows only the COPY command. The final image contains only bcrypt-derived hashes.
phase_12_subwave_a_final_architecture.validation_hook: tests/test_ntfy_health.py::test_ntfy_prebaked_users_via_docker_exec — behavioral check inside the running container: `docker exec infotriage-ntfy ntfy user list --auth-file /etc/ntfy/auth.db --json` MUST report both `producer` and `reader` users. Works against either build substrate (archived sealed-bind-mount OR current Dockerfile pre-bake) since both place a bcrypt-only auth.db at the canonical path.

non_goals: Do NOT re-open the sealed-bind-mount attempt — it is archived with a tombstone; the 298-line scripts/seed_ntfy_sealed.py is not a future path.
Do NOT hand-edit the image-layered /etc/ntfy/auth.db. Re-bake via `make ntfy-build` (idempotent; layer cache invalidates when any of the 3 secrets changes).
Do NOT add an entrypoint: or command: override to the ntfy service block. ADR-018 is pre-bake; the container just runs ntfy serve.
Do NOT cite Phase 999.2 / mE5-large / cross-language entity linking as Phase 12 scope. Those are unrelated to alerting.
Do NOT pre-release to production until ADR-017 Decision 2 (image pin) and Decision 3 (commit-time secret scanner) ship as a follow-up ADR.
enforcement: HANDOFF.json phase_12_subwave_a_audit_findings.scope_lock.decision = SUPERSEDED_BY_PIVOT — visible to any /gsd-progress --next dispatch.
docs/adr/ADR-018-phase-12-dockerfile-buildkit-secrets.md — Status: Accepted; the new authoritative substrate.
apps/ntfy/Dockerfile — 37-line multi-stage; sealed :ro bind mount removed; only COPY /etc/ntfy/auth.db.
docker-compose.yml ntfy block — `build:` + top-level `secrets:` block + cache `:rw`; no host-side script.
tests/test_ntfy_health.py — 6 tests (5 existing behavioral + 1 new docker-exec behavioral); no file-system artifact coupling.
Makefile — ntfy-build is the only legitimate way to populate auth.db; ntfy-up auto-fires if image missing.
docs/planning/phase-12-subwave-a-audit.md — SUPERSEDED banner at top documenting the audit→pivot history.
docs/planning/_archived/phase-12-sealed-bind-mount-attempt/ — preserved for historical reference; README explains why superseded.

phase_13_ingest_body_wiring.note: apps/ingest-{gmail,imap,youtube,telegram,barentswatch,acled,obsidian} currently UPSERT only body_ref. To populate the new body TEXT column, each ingest service needs a parallel write in PostgresStore.put_item. Until then production articles.body stays NULL and the link view reads summary-only.
phase_13_ingest_body_wiring.scope_lock_impact: Sub-wave (f) Phase 13 body wiring ships INSIDE Phase 12 (per phase_12_subwave_a_audit_findings.ship_next.sub_wave_f). It is NOT a separate milestone phase.

phase_12_phase_13_depend.verified_by: post-ADR-015 ordering-validation pass (gsd-progress --next dispatch; phase_12_phase_13_depend ask)
phase_12_phase_13_depend.do_not_flip_ordering_rationale: Phase 12 alerting has no structural dependency on Phase 13. ADR-015 Decision 3 payload derives from enrichment row fields + summary only; deep_link targets Obsidian vault, NOT the link view.
phase_12_phase_13_depend.soft_dependency_detail: Link view (apps/brief/views.py?view=links) reads body via render_links(). Until Phase 13 lands, all body fields are NULL → link view URL extraction is summary-only and under-extracts outbound URLs.
phase_12_phase_13_depend.operational_impact: CAT I hits are rare (~1-5/day typical); operator's primary action is the Obsidian deep-link. Link view is downstream reading-queue surface visited AFTER reading the alert item; degraded link-view URL coverage is recovered (not lost) when the operator reads via Obsidian directly.
phase_12_phase_13_depend.do_flip_ordering_if: Operator flags the link view as a CRITICAL CAT I downstream surface (workflow `ntfy -> link view` rather than `ntfy -> Obsidian note`). If so, ADR-016 supersession at discuss-phase Turn-2.

DELETED originals: scripts/seed_ntfy_sealed.py, configs/ntfy-sealed/{server.yml, .gitkeep}, docs/adr/ADR-017-sealed-bind-mount-addendum.md.

Live operator flow validation (non-blocking but recommended): `make ntfy-build && make ntfy-up && make ntfy-publish-test` confirms BuildKit secrets did pre-bake producer + reader into the auth.db of the runtime image. The 5 expected-failures in tests/test_ntfy_health.py should flip to passing.

decisions.rationale: Real mE5-large vector re-validation (backlog 999.3) recommended 0.92 via conservative overmerge==0 fallback.
Entity Graph.md projection should show actual aliases (mention + lang) rather than a bare count.

decisions.decision: Row-based and store-backed Entity Graph both render type and language
Entity NER in triage worker guarded by asyncio.wait_for timeout
decisions.rationale: Prevents hung LLM NER call from blocking verdict.ready per ADR-004 R5 prohibition.
Enables --corpus-from-postgres to pair Cyrillic and Latin mentions of the same entity; live run validated at T*=0.92 (GO).

decisions.decision: Sealed-bind-mount replaces in-container ACL bootstrap for Phase 12 sub-wave (a)
decisions.rationale: 9 rounds of iterating on the in-container shell-script bootstrap were brittle-system-thrashing; the architectural shift to sealed-bind-mount (host-side one-time prep + bind :ro) is the correct foundation. Locks phase_12_subwave_a_audit_findings.scope_lock.decision = LOCKED.

decisions.decision: Phase 13 body-wiring ships as Phase 12 sub-wave (f)
decisions.rationale: Per phase_12_phase_13_depend verdict INTEGRATED-SUB-WAVE; alert payload never reads body. Bundling avoids cross-phase ordering confusion.

decisions.decision: ADR-018 Dockerfile pre-bake + BuildKit secrets is the final Phase 12 sub-wave (a) substrate
decisions.rationale: Sealed-bind-mount architecture (commit 5c52056 + ADR-017 addendum) was operational but still overcomplicated for the ntfy single-binary: 298-line host-side Python prelude + sealed config file + bind :ro pattern. ADR-018 collapses it to 1 custom Dockerfile (37 lines) that pre-bakes /etc/ntfy/auth.db at image-build time via BuildKit secrets; plaintext passwords never land in image layers (only bcrypt hashes). Compose passes the secrets via its native secrets: {environment: ...} source syntax. The 4 LOCKED ADR-017 ACL decisions are preserved verbatim — only the implementation substrate changed. Locks HANDOFF.json phase_12_subwave_a_audit_findings.scope_lock.decision = SUPERSEDED_BY_PIVOT.
decisions.supersedes_prior_decision: Sealed-bind-mount replaces in-container ACL bootstrap for Phase 12 sub-wave (a) (locked 2026-07-23); the lock is now superseded_by_pivot

decisions_recorded_this_audit.rationale: 9 rounds of bootstrap scripting land in sealed-bind-mount architecture (ADR-017 sealed-bind-mount addendum). Brittleness enumerated in phase_12_subwave_a_audit_findings.brittle_decisions; ship-next directives point at sub-waves (b)+(c)+(d) on the rebuilt foundation.

prerequisite_commits.purpose: feat(ingest-youtube): Phase 11 Wave 5 — local faster-whisper transcription

next_action: Phase 12 sub-wave (a) PIVOT locked on Dockerfile pre-bake (ADR-018). HANDOFF.json phase_12_subwave_a_final_architecture.architecture + .ship_next are the dispatch target. Phase 12 continues with sub-waves (b) outbox+DLX, (c) payload emitter, (d) throttling, (e) failure-mode tests, (f) Phase 13 body wiring — all per phase_12_subwave_a_final_architecture.ship_next. Begin via /gsd-execute-phase 12 (or whichever GSD dispatch surfaces next). Sub-wave (a) is closed for further development (sealed-bind-mount is archived; ADR-018 is the substrate).
context_notes: Phase 12 sub-wave (a) overran 9 rounds of in-container bootstrap debugging, was pivoted to sealed-bind-mount (commit 5c52056), and was finally pivoted to Dockerfile pre-bake + BuildKit secrets (ADR-018, 2026-07-23). The sealed-bind-mount audit remains as historical record at phase_12_subwave_a_audit_findings (scope_lock=deprecated/SUPERSEDED_BY_PIVOT). The 4 LOCKED ADR-017 ACL decisions are preserved across both pivots. The audit-and-pivot cycle generalizes: brittle-system thrashing has no terminus without a widely different substrate.

adr_004_resolved.historical_note: ADR-004 was never committed to docs/adr/.
